items.title: After her husband's death, Annie discovered he had placed 100 bets a day

children.content: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.

articles.title: Advocates say 'three big gorillas' have stopped real national gambling reform

children.content: Under the cover of budget day, the Albanese government this week dropped its long-awaited response to a gambling harm inquiry. Advocates say it has "failed miserably".

image.altText: A Manly Sea Eagles jersey with rainbow bands above and below the sponsor name and on the cuffs and collar

A close up of a woman starring off to the left with a serious look on her face

items.title: Most widespread May rain in a decade to reach all states

children.content: The most widespread May rain in a decade is about to sweep across Australia as the first north-west cloudband of 2026 becomes a reality.

image.altText: heavy dark clouds over the Clarence River in the NSW North Coast

items.title: Wayne Swan slams 'view from Mar-a-Lago' as Labor faces another test

children.content: Labor and the Coalition are both acutely mindful of One Nation's rise and its determination to take a decisive role in a future conservative government.

image.altText: A man with glasses and grey hair

items.title: The most difficult question faced by antisemitism royal commission

children.content: Jewish Australians told the royal commission that antisemitism has spread into everyday life. But whether the inquiry can handle what it has promised to confront remains to be seen.

image.altText: People walk into a building where police are present for the royal commission.

items.title: Netanyahu's attempts to corral the UAE put Middle East politics on display

children.content: The question for the Gulf states is whether they are happy for Israel to be the dominant player in the Middle East.

image.altText: Benjamin Netanyahu at a press conference in front of an Israeli flag.

items.title: Trump and Xi's tea in Beijing rewrites the century

children.content: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

articles.title: No major Iran war breakthroughs as Trump leaves Beijing

children.content: US President Donald Trump left China on Friday without securing tangible help from Beijing to end the Iran war, but praised China's President, Xi Jinping, during his visit.

image.altText: Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump walk in blue suits in front of five American and Chinese flags.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

items.title: Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something bigger

children.content: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

image.altText: A graphic showing an ocean cutaway with satellites, buouys, drones and sensors trying to detect a submarine.

items.title: 'Grossly incompetent': Action taken against rogue MP after online outburst

children.content: Contempt proceedings have been launched against Mark Latham after he attacked a tribunal that found he unlawfully vilified and sexually harassed another state MP.

image.altText: Mark Latham in a black coat and orange time walking

items.title: A house fire cost Patrick and Susan everything, including their two dogs

children.content: Patrick and Susan Doyle recently lost almost everything in a house fire, including their beloved dogs, Oscar and Mercy. While the couple escaped with their lives, Mrs Doyle was badly burnt. They are warning others to be better prepared.

gallery.altText: Two dogs standing in front of a car
firefighters extinguishing a house fire, with red flames and smoke in the background
A couple taking a selfie at a restaurant

thumbnail.altText: Two dogs standing in front of a car
firefighters extinguishing a house fire, with red flames and smoke in the background
A couple taking a selfie at a restaurant

items.title: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools

children.content: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."

image.altText: Jane Caro

items.title: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group

children.content: The leader of Neo-Nazi group National Socialist Network, Thomas Sewell, has appeared in an interview online alongside notorious US Neo-Nazi James Mason, who is listed as a terrorist entity by the Canadian government.

image.altText: Shadowy figures in the foreground on an orange background with an Australian flag and US flag

items.title: The secret life of flowers revealed in stunning photos

children.content: Have you ever looked at a flower up close?  Or wondered how plants are pollinated? Take a journey through the secret life of plants captured in stunning images by Australian botanists.

gallery.altText: Close up of a flower with white petals and bright yellow anthers at its centre.
Close-up of golden grains of pollen coming from tip of a golden flower.
Close up of yellow flower anthers.

items.title: $40m bid for airline crackdown with warnings of fare rises, cancelled flights

children.content: The federal government has announced nearly $40 million to fund an overhaul of aviation consumer rights.

image.altText: A woman stands at the top level taking a selfie looking down at airport check-in

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

items.title: A little box of joy came into my life at just the right time

children.content: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.

gallery.altText: Lapis the bluetit in her nest

thumbnail.altText: Lapis the bluetit in her nest

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Nic Cage makes the leap to TV in Spider-Man spin off

children.content: Plus, Shaun Micallef dives into Australia's gambling problem and the Stranger Things creators back another sci-fi mystery.

image.altText: A man in a black costume crouches on a desk

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a 90s club banger

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

image.altText: A dog is blessed by a priest in Spain on Saint Anthony's Day.

items.title: Video of 4m tiger shark beheading prompts call for change

children.content: A researcher speaks out against the fishing of sharks after footage of a huge tiger shark being caught and decapitated circulated on social media.

gallery.altText: hands holding large shark's mouth open.

thumbnail.altText: hands holding large shark's mouth open.

avatar.alt: Will Corbett standing in centre frame looking straight towards camera

items.title: Judge declares another mistrial in Harvey Weinstein rape case

children.content: Harvey Weinstein's third trial in New York on a charge that he raped aspiring actress Jessica Mann has ended in a mistrial, after a jury failed to reach a unanimous verdict.

image.altText: A man in a suit and glasses with facial hair sits in a courtroom

items.title: Zelenskyy vows retribution against Russia for missile strike that killed 24

children.content: Russia has unleashed its heaviest bombardment of the Ukrainian capital this year after a US-brokered ceasefire ended.

image.altText: A woman cries as she lays flowers at a memorial.

items.title: Israel, Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire despite new strikes

children.content: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire and hold new talks on a political settlement, even as Israel carried out new strikes it insists are not subject to the truce.

image.altText: Smoke billows from a village

items.title: Five die following cave diving incident

children.content: The group was exploring a series of underwater caves at a depth of about 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll in the Maldives.

image.altText: Side-by-side images of a mother (left) and her daughter.

items.title: Dylan Pietsch is a world-class rugby winger. He's also an inspiring artist

children.content: While Dylan Pietsch is a star on the rugby field for the Wallabies and Western Force, he turns to his love of painting to express himself and be creative.

gallery.altText: Dylan Pietsch celebrates a Wallabies try
Two brightly coloured Indigenous dot paintings.

thumbnail.altText: Dylan Pietsch celebrates a Wallabies try
Two brightly coloured Indigenous dot paintings.

items.title: 'Cricket is a funny game': Cruel dismissal denies Marsh IPL century

children.content: Mitch Marsh receives a nightmare slice of luck after a stunning innings put him on course for the triple figures.

image.altText: Mitch Marsh plays a shot through the off side

items.title: Aussies climb into podium contention as Vingegaard claims win

children.content: Jonas Vingegaard stamped his authority on the Giro d'Italia, but three Aussies are in the top 10 overall and all remain in consideration for the podium.

image.altText: Winner Jonas Vinegaard in yellow cycling suit holds up a giant wine glass in celebration.

items.title: 'Recipe for disaster': Road safety expert 'not surprised' by fatal Qld bus crash

children.content: A retired traffic engineer says Australia is "decades behind" in road safety standards and needs an overhaul after a second fatal bus crash on the same stretch of the Bruce Highway.

image.altText: A green bus on its side in grass after a crash. Emergency service workers are seen on the right hand side.

items.title: Voters head to polls for Stafford by-election

children.content: Labor candidate Luke Richmond wants to make the contest a referendum on health, while LNP candidate Fiona Hammond is focussed on fuel, the cost of living, and crime.

image.altText: A graphic showing Fiona Hammond and Luke Richmond with blue and red in the background as well as Brisbane's skyline.

items.title: Whether they're faded, frayed or stained, every hat tells a story

children.content: For many, hats are more than a way to keep the sun off your face; they tell stories and hold memories from far and wide. Five men share their collections.

gallery.altText: Man stacking his hat collection in a shed.
Inside an old, moldy hat.
Close up of a North Queensland Cowboys cap with a radio build into it.

items.title: Natural burials offer families peace at NSW conservation cemetery

children.content: After nearly 10 years getting public health and council approvals, a natural burial ground on the NSW south coast begins letting people have a simpler, more environmentally friendly farewell.

image.altText: A grey haired woman smiles towards the camera, holding a photo of her adult son in one hand and a red banksia in the other

items.title: Scott Morrison joins historic bronze collection in avenue of honour

children.content: A thinner ex-PM cracks jokes as his bust is revealed, making him the 30th Australian leader with a likeness in Ballarat's Prime Ministers Avenue.

image.altText: Scott Morrison standing next to a bronze bust of himself.

items.title: First memorial to Australia's deadliest Vietnam War battle unveiled

children.content: The longest and deadliest battle for Australian troops in the Vietnam War has finally been memorialised with a statue to the Battle of Coral–Balmoral erected in Gundagai.

image.altText: a bronze statue of two men in army uniforms, one carrying the other in a park

items.title: From colonisation to 'safe space': The story of Dhirri-aay-aay

children.content: Back on country, Aunty Polly Cutmore says she feels like a child again when visiting her ancestral lands. Now, as a respected Goombri elder, she shares the stories of her country.

image.altText: An older women smiles off camera, you can see the joy in her eyes, she wears a blue shirt and has big white curly hair

items.title: Why clothes shop that 'wouldn't be out of place in Sydney' is in Goondiwindi

children.content: Queensland cotton farmer and entrepreneur Sam Coulton has spent decades diversifying beyond agriculture, building a fashion brand and tourism business in Goondiwindi while creating jobs and investing in the future of his rural town.

A man standing in a cotton field.
The interior of a clothing store with mannequins and racks.

items.title: New Hollywood film excites as Philippine culture hits the big screen

children.content: As animated film Forgotten Island gets ready to hit the big screen later this year, could it signal an end to Filipino typecasting in Hollywood?
